metadata.task: Drop the ball and determine the coefficient of restitution from the observed rebound height compared with the starting height. Output one number rounded to the nearest tenth.
metadata.description: Use the ball and ground in the simulator to measure the initial peak height and the first rebound peak. Compute the coefficient from those observed heights instead of relying on a preset text value.

expected_behavior: The LLM calculates the coefficient of restitution by comparing the heights of consecutive bounces.
reasoning: The coefficient of restitution is sqrt(h2 / h1) = sqrt(1.28 / 2.0) = 0.8.
